explanation: The requirement states the system shall provide means to switch from PCV to PSV while PCV ventilation is active. Translating this, 'PCVMode' represents that PCV ventilation is active and 'PSVModeSelected' indicates the user initiates the switch to PSV. The requirement specifies providing the ability/means—not that the switch must occur automatically, only that it's permitted/possible. In LTL, this is best captured by asserting that, whenever the system is in PCVMode, it is always possible to eventually select PSVMode (i.e., the user can initiate a switch). This is captured by G(PCVMode -> F(PSVModeSelected)): globally, if we are in PCV mode, it is always possible (eventually) to select PSV mode. Previous LTL formulas that might insist on an automatic switch, or require PSVMode to follow PCVMode immediately, would be too strong or not allow for this possibility during PCVMode, hence this formula is most accurate.

The requirement is that, while PCV ventilation is currently active (represented by the atomic proposition PCVMode), the system must provide a means to switch to PSV (represented by the initiation of a switch, captured as PSVModeSelected). In LTL, this is best formulated as: whenever the system is in PCV mode (globally, whenever PCVMode holds), it should always be possible that eventually the user can initiate the switch to PSV (i.e., eventually PSVModeSelected becomes true, while PCVMode is still true). This property is captured by the LTL formula: G (PCVMode -> F (PSVModeSelected)). This ensures that at any point during PCV ventilation, the option to switch to PSV will eventually be provided (can occur in the future). Previous properties that express, for example, that 'PCVMode implies PSVModeSelected' would be too strict, as it would mean the switch must occur immediately, which is not required by the original natural language.

The natural language states that the system shall provide a way to switch from PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) while PCV ventilation is active. The relevant atomic propositions are PCVMode (indicating PCV ventilation active) and PSVModeSelected (indicating the switch to PSV mode is initiated by the user). The LTL property should guarantee that whenever PCVMode is active, it is always possible (i.e., eventually, at some point in the future, while in PCVMode) to trigger "PSVModeSelected," representing the means to switch being present. Thus, globally, whenever PCVMode is true, it should eventually be possible to select PSVMode, formulated as G(PCVMode -> F(PSVModeSelected)). This LTL property captures the requirement that as long as PCV ventilation is active, there is always a possibility to initiate a switch to PSV, in accordance with the input statement.

The requirement states that the system must allow a mode switch from PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) while PCV ventilation is active. In the available atomic propositions, we have 'PCVMode' to indicate the system is currently in PCV mode and 'PSVModeSelected' to indicate the user has initiated a switch to PSV mode. The LTL property globally asserts that whenever the system is in PCVMode, it is always possible (eventually) for 'PSVModeSelected' to occur, meaning the user can initiate a switch to PSV at any point during PCV mode. This captures the intent that the system 'shall provide means to switch from PCV to PSV while PCV is active.'

The requirement states that while PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) mode (PCVMode) is active, the system must provide the means to switch to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation), which can be interpreted as making the 'Switch to PSV' action possible while PCVMode is active. The atomic proposition 'PSVModeSelected' indicates the user has initiated a switch to PSV mode, and 'PCVMode' indicates PCV ventilation is active. We want to capture that whenever PCVMode is true, it remains possible at any moment to switch to PSV (initiate PSVModeSelected). This possibility is captured by the formula globally (G), for all times when PCVMode is active, it is always possible to eventually select PSVMode; i.e., G (PCVMode -> F PSVModeSelected).

The natural language requirement states that while PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) is active, there must be a mechanism to switch to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation). Focusing on the atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' represents PCV ventilation being active, and 'PSVModeSelected' represents the initiation of a switch to PSV mode. Although actual 'means to switch' may be more complex in practice, with just these atomic propositions, the requirement can be formalized as: 'Whenever PCV mode is active, it is always possible to eventually initiate the switch to PSV mode.' In LTL, this is captured by: G(PCVMode -> F(PSVModeSelected)). This means that at all times when PCVMode is active, it must remain possible in the future to select PSVMode. If there were prior LTLs such as 'F(PSVModeSelected)' (eventually switched to PSV) or 'PCVMode -> PSVModeSelected' (immediately must switch), those do not capture the continuous availability of switching whenever PCVMode is active. The proposed property captures the ongoing obligation given the system's state.

The natural language requirement states that there must be a way to switch from PCV (Pressure Controlled Ventilation) to PSV (Pressure Support Ventilation) while PCV ventilation is active. In terms of atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' indicates PCV ventilation is active, and 'PSVModeSelected' indicates a user-initiated switch to PSV mode. The LTL formula 'G (PCVMode -> F PSVModeSelected)' expresses that whenever the system is in PCV mode, it should eventually be possible to initiate a switch to PSV mode—thus, providing the means to switch modes while PCV is active. Previous formulas (if any) may not explicitly require that the means exist during PCVMode or may not formalize the requirement of the transition possibility while PCVMode is active.

The natural language requirement states that while PCV ventilation is active, the system shall provide means to switch to PSV. In terms of LTL with the provided atomic propositions, we can interpret 'PCV ventilation is active' as 'PCVMode', and 'means to switch from PCV to PSV' as the ability for the user to select switching to PSV ('PSVModeSelected'). Therefore, we require that whenever the system is in PCVMode, it must always eventually be possible for PSVModeSelected to occur (i.e., the switch to PSV can be requested). This property is expressed with the LTL formula: G(PCVMode -> F(PSVModeSelected)). This guarantees that any time PCVMode holds, there is a future state in which PSVModeSelected is true, thereby fulfilling the requirement that switching is always possible while PCV is active. No previously given LTL properties are present, so there is no conflict or alternative to consider.

The requirement states that the system must provide a way to switch from Pressure Controlled Ventilation (PCV) to Pressure Support Ventilation (PSV) while PCV ventilation is active. Within the available atomic propositions, 'PCVMode' and 'PSVModeSelected' are most relevant: 'PCVMode' indicates PCV ventilation is active, and 'PSVModeSelected' indicates the initiation of a switch to PSV mode. The specification is about the continuous availability (at all times) of the possibility to switch when in PCV mode. In LTL, this is best captured by stating that globally (G), whenever PCVMode holds, it is possible (i.e., eventually) for the user to select PSV mode ('PSVModeSelected'). Thus, 'G( PCVMode -> F PSVModeSelected )' means: whenever the system is in PCV mode, it is always eventually possible to initiate a switch to PSV mode (i.e., the means to switch is provided during PCV operation). This captures the main intent of the requirement, which emphasizes the availability of the switch during PCV, not forcing it to occur, but making it always possible.

The requirement specifies that while PCV ventilation is active (i.e., PCVMode holds), there shall be a means to switch to PSV (i.e., the user selects or initiates the PSV mode, represented by PSVModeSelected). Since 'provide means to switch' does not specify that the switch should actually occur, but simply that it must be possible (i.e., the action is enabled or not forbidden at any time PCV is active), this is best captured as: 'Globally, whenever the system is in PCVMode, it is always possible to switch to PSV.' In LTL, this is classically written as: G(PCVMode -> F(PSVModeSelected)), meaning that at all times, if PCVMode holds, there will exist a future point (possibly immediately) where PSVModeSelected can occur. This captures the natural language because it ensures that as long as PCV ventilation is active, the ability to select/invoke a transition to PSV is always guaranteed. No previous LTL given. No numerical comparisons are used; only atomic propositions and temporal and boolean operators.
